deep_dive.title: Attention-Free and Lightweight Token Reduction for Efficient Vision-Language Models
deep_dive.abstract: Vision-Language Models (VLMs) have achieved strong performance in multimodal understanding, yet remain challenging to deploy on resource-constrained edge devices due to the substantial computational overhead of processing numerous visual tokens. Token reduction is a promising direction for accelerating VLMs inference, but existing approaches either rely on attention maps that are incompatible with modern acceleration frameworks or depend on computationally intensive pairwise similarity comparisons, which undermine scalability and negate their practical benefits in deployment. In this paper, we propose an attention-free and lightweight token reduction framework as a plug-and-play module for VLMs, which preserves both important and diverse tokens to produce a compact visual representation. First, to enable attention-free importance estimation, we adopt an information-theoretic perspective and quantify token information using a novel entropy-based criterion, retaining those with more expressive and less degenerate feature representations. Second, to ensure diverse visual coverage in a lightweight manner, we introduce a transformation-induced consistency signal where similar tokens yield similar signals, such that sorting by this signal places similar tokens close to each other and enables stride-based selection to produce a diverse token set. Extensive experiments across multiple VLMs benchmarks demonstrate that our framework achieves a favorable accuracy-efficiency trade-off, maintaining competitive performance under aggressive compression.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: token reduction for efficient vision-language models

deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Proposes an attention-free, lightweight token reduction framework for VLMs that preserves important and diverse tokens, enabling efficient multimodal understanding on edge devices.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 该论文提出一种无注意力、轻量级的token缩减框架，作为即插即用模块用于视觉语言模型（VLM）。它不依赖注意力图或成对相似度计算，通过保留重要且多样化的token来加速推理。在多个VLM基准上，该方法在保持性能的同时显著降低了计算量，支持在资源受限设备上部署多模态理解模型。

deep_dive.matched_query_text: Papers central to 多模态大模型相关论文，包括理解、检索、幻觉等方向, especially work that connects or combines: multimodal understanding models; multimodal hallucination detection; multimodal reasoning tasks; papers on multimodal understanding in large language models; causes and mitigation of hallucination in multimodal models; architecture of large multimodal models combining vision and language.

quick_skim.title: Geometry-aware Gaussian Prior and Axial Attention for Cervical Cytology Image Classification
quick_skim.abstract: Accurate cervical cytology image classification is a key component of automated cervical cancer screening, where reliable recognition of normal, precancerous, and cancer-associated cellular patterns from Pap smear images can improve screening efficiency and diagnostic consistency. However, this task remains challenging because cervical cells exhibit complex morphology, subtle intra-class variations, and strong inter-class similarities. Existing convolution-based models capture local texture well but have limited ability to model long-range relationships, whereas attention-based models provide broader context but often lack explicit structural guidance. To address these limitations, we propose a geometry-aware classification framework for cervical cancer screening-oriented cytology image analysis, incorporating semantic abstraction and structural priors learned from pre-trained vision-language features. The method uses Gaussian expert modules to generate axis-wise priors from global semantic information, capturing structural regularities such as nuclear alignment and cellular spatial organization. These priors are embedded into an axial self-attention module to modulate similarity computation along horizontal and vertical directions, improving long-range dependency modeling and structure-sensitive feature interaction. Experiments on the Mendeley liquid-based cytology and SIPaKMeD datasets show that the proposed method achieves 99.48% accuracy on the former and 96.08% on the latter, with balanced gains in recall, precision, and overall classification performance. Visual analysis further shows that the learned priors highlight diagnostically relevant cellular regions, demonstrating the potential of the proposed framework as a screening-oriented decision-support tool for cervical cytology.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: axial attention for long-range dependencies in cervical cytology classification
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 轴向注意力用于宫颈细胞学分类中的长程依赖建模

quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 轴向注意力用于宫颈细胞学分类中的长程依赖建模
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Integrates axial attention (sparse pattern) with geometric Gaussian prior for accurate cervical cytology classification, improving long-range modeling.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 宫颈细胞学图像分类中，卷积缺乏长程关系而注意力缺乏结构先验。提出结合几何感知高斯先验与轴向注意力的框架，轴向注意力作为一种稀疏模式，分别沿行和列捕获全局依赖，同时利用高斯先验建模细胞结构。实验证明该方法有效提升了分类准确率，展示了稀疏注意力在医疗图像中的应用价值。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 宫颈细胞学图像分类中，卷积缺乏长程关系而注意力缺乏结构先验。提出结合几何感知高斯先验与轴向注意力的框架，轴向注意力作为一种稀疏模式，分别沿行和列捕获全局依赖，同时利用高斯先验建模细胞结构。实验证明该方法有效提升了分类准确率，展示了稀疏注意力在医疗图像中的应用价值。

quick_skim.title: VisCo: Leveraging Large Language Models as Intrinsic Encoders for Visual Token Compression
quick_skim.abstract: Vision-language models (VLMs) process large numbers of visual tokens, resulting in substantial inference latency and memory overhead. This has motivated extensive research on visual token compression. While training-free strategies rely on heuristic metrics and suffer significant performance degradation under high compression ratios, many training-based methods introduce external compression modules that force the VLM backbone to adapt, incurring substantial retraining cost and compromising VLMs' priors. Effective visual token compression hinges on strong information encoding, a capability already present in pretrained VLMs but underutilized by existing approaches. Motivated by this, we propose VisCo, a training-efficient self-compression framework that reuses the pretrained VLM itself as an intrinsic compressor. VisCo is a parameter-sharing autoencoder that compresses visual information using a small set of memory tokens and transfers hierarchical information from encoding to decoding. Experiments show that VisCo surpasses prior methods across all evaluated compression ratios, with larger gains under more aggressive compression, and remains stable even in the extreme single-token setting. Moreover, when combined with the original visual tokens, the learned memory tokens can even improve the base model, suggesting that VisCo captures complementary representations beyond compression.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: visual token compression using LLM intrinsic encoding for efficiency
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 利用LLM内在编码能力进行视觉令牌压缩提升效率
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 利用LLM内在编码能力进行视觉令牌压缩提升效率
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 利用LLM内在编码能力进行视觉令牌压缩提升效率
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: VisCo leverages pretrained LLMs as intrinsic compressors to reduce visual tokens, achieving high compression ratios without retraining the VLM backbone.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 针对视觉语言模型中视觉标记数量大导致推理慢和显存高的问题，提出VisCo，利用预训练大语言模型内在的编码能力对视觉标记进行自我压缩，避免了引入外部模块和重新训练骨干。实验表明，在保持性能的同时实现高压缩比，为高效多模态推理提供了训练高效的方案。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 针对视觉语言模型中视觉标记数量大导致推理慢和显存高的问题，提出VisCo，利用预训练大语言模型内在的编码能力对视觉标记进行自我压缩，避免了引入外部模块和重新训练骨干。实验表明，在保持性能的同时实现高压缩比，为高效多模态推理提供了训练高效的方案。
quick_skim.matched_query_text: recent advances in efficient attention mechanisms and memory compression
